profile.primary_purpose: Drive downloads and adoption of Warp terminal as the premier platform for building with AI coding agents
profile.brand_signature: Dark, immersive interface with deep charcoal backgrounds and atmospheric textural hero imagery featuring aurora-like gradients. Clean, minimal typography with Matter headings paired with Inter body text creates a premium developer tool aesthetic that emphasizes clarity and precision.

visual_language.accent_strategy: Subtle tertiary purple accent (--color-tertiary) used sparingly for progress indicators and interactive states; primary palette relies on warm neutrals (olive tones) against deep dark backgrounds
visual_language.highlights: Atmospheric gradient overlays on hero imagery with radial light effects; product screenshots with subtle ring borders for definition

target_audience: Senior software engineers and developers who work heavily in terminal environments and seek productivity improvements
Engineering teams at tech companies evaluating AI-powered development tools for code review and automation
DevOps and infrastructure engineers managing cloud deployments who need efficient CLI workflows
Technical leaders and CTOs exploring enterprise-ready AI agent platforms for their organizations
strategy.executive_synthesis: Warp's visual identity strategically positions the product at the intersection of familiar developer tooling and cutting-edge AI capabilities. The dark, terminal-native aesthetic immediately signals to developers that this is a tool built for them, while the atmospheric hero imagery and premium typography elevate it beyond typical CLI tools. The multi-agent tabbed showcase (Warp Agent, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini) communicates platform versatility and openness. Enterprise trust signals through the logo bar featuring GitHub, Amazon, and Nvidia, combined with enterprise navigation, support both individual adoption and organizational purchasing decisions.

style_classification.reasoning: The visual impression from the screenshot immediately signals a dark, futuristic, developer-focused aesthetic. The hero section features a dramatic aurora gradient (pink, purple, teal, cyan) with particle effects against pure black backgrounds—this is the quintessential "neon on dark" Cyberpunk signature. The color profile confirms this: dominant #121212 backgrounds with vibrant accent purples (#CBB0F7), teals, and pinks creating high contrast neon glows. The typography uses Sans-Serif Modern (Inter) and Monospace (DM Mono) for code/terminal elements—both explicitly listed as Cyberpunk type characteristics. The layout is Grid-Based Strict with card-based sections and dense information architecture (security features, integrations, download options), matching Cyberpunk's Dense/Packed layout profile. The glass blur effects (backdrop-filter: blur) and linear gradients in the surfaces profile add to the high-tech, futuristic feel. The overall mood is dystopian-adjacent tech: terminal windows, code editors, AI agents, and developer tooling presented with a premium, cutting-edge visual language.
style_classification.reasoning_secondary: The Corporate modifier applies because despite the bold Cyberpunk aesthetic, the design maintains enterprise credibility signals: prominent logo bar featuring major tech companies (DoorDash, OpenAI, GitHub, Amazon, Anthropic), a dedicated "Designed for security. Built for organizations" section emphasizing SOC 2 compliance and enterprise controls, and structured feature grids with icon+text patterns common in B2B SaaS. The typography hierarchy is professional and readable (not chaotic), the layout follows clean 12-column grids with generous whitespace between sections, and the overall information architecture prioritizes trust and competence—hallmarks of Corporate style. This is Cyberpunk filtered through enterprise design requirements.

layout.section_separation: Sections are separated by generous vertical whitespace (py-[var(--space-section)], approximately 4rem × density multiplier). Alternating background colors between pure black (#000000) and very dark charcoal (#121212). Some sections have gradient backgrounds (hero, agent workflows) transitioning from teal to purple. No visible divider lines or borders between sections—separation achieved through whitespace and subtle background color shifts. Cards and feature sections use dark surfaces (#1C1C1C, #2C2B29) that float against the black background with subtle borders (rgba(255,255,255,0.1)).

**NAV:**
- Background: Dark navy/charcoal (#0D1117 approx)

- Buttons/CTAs: "Download ▼" (white text, dropdown button, right-aligned)

**HERO:**
- Background: Vibrant aurora gradient (pink, purple, teal, cyan) with particle/snow effects
- Main headline: Large white text, bold weight
- Terminal mockup: Dark-themed code editor overlaid on gradient

- Search bar: White/light with subtle shadow

**FEATURE GRID (3x2):**

**INTEGRATIONS:**

**GET WARP CTA:**

**OVERALL THEME:**

- Accent colors: Purple (#8B5CF6), Teal (#14B8A6), Pink (#FF1CF7)

- Imagery: Terminal/code mockups, abstract gradients, particle effects
- Mood: Developer-focused, futuristic, AI-powered, premium

rules.description: Primary page background and terminal foundation.
Secondary background for raised elements, panels, and cards.
Default border color separating major structural elements.
Primary high-contrast text color for strong legibility on dark modes.
Standard reading text, softer than absolute white to lower eye strain.
Brand accent color specifically identifying the AI/Agent capabilities.
Used to signal success statuses and generic product differentiation gradients.
Highlights and alternate tier brand differentiation.
Atmospheric gradient background for alternative hero zones.
Colors governing syntactical representation and custom code block embeds.

insights: High-contrast dark theme driven by #121212 backgrounds with muted off-white text (#F8F5EE, #C0BDB8) to reduce eye strain, emphasizing a focus-oriented developer aesthetic.
Accents are functionally segmented utilizing vibrant tones like Electric Purple (#CBB0F7) and Emerald Green (#00884B) to differentiate feature offerings (e.g. Agentic logic vs generic tooling).
Extensive use of low-opacity blacks and whites over solid colors allows gradients and glows to bleed through the background UI, reinforcing depth seamlessly.

paragraphs.description: Body typography variants optimized for interface legibility, features distinct tracking and spacing profiles suitable for dense product interfaces.

labels.description: Utilitarian typography used for badges, code execution strings, captions, and micro-labels.

others.description: Additional typography tokens available in the CSS infrastructure but not primarily classified into standard flows.

typography.highlights: Interactive typography states feature a "link-underline-reveal", while secondary labels use a high-contrast badge treatment ("bg-[var(--color-text)]/10 text-[var(--color-text)]").
insights: The system implements a fluid, responsive scale for headings using `clamp()` combined with viewport units and CSS variables (e.g., `clamp(2.5rem, 5vw, var(--heading-size))`), ensuring typography scales seamlessly across screen sizes.

There is a strict functional division of typefaces: 'Matter' is reserved entirely for display and primary headings (H1-H3), 'Inter' provides extreme legibility for body passages and UI/labels, while 'DM Mono' handles technical microcopy and code snippets.
Small categorical labels and eyebrows use 'DM Mono' (12px) paired with an uppercase transformation and wide tracking (0.05em) to establish clear hierarchy without competing with standard headings.

highlights: Backdrop blur effects on navigation (blur(10px) and blur(36px) with saturation(150%))
Glass panel surfaces in navigation flyouts with backdrop-blur-md
Radial spotlight gradient overlay on hero section
Animated 4-color border gradient (gold/magenta/geekblue/cyan) with 400% background-size animation
Linear gradient masks for horizontal scrolling logo carousel (transparent edges)
Soft black/5 overlay on hero background image
Ring inset borders with black/10 and white/10 opacity on images

elevation.description: Elevation allows elements to be lifted from the background. It can be defined by applying drop shadows or other visual cues, such as color and borders

shadows.description: Elevation using shadows reflects a spatial relationship by indicating separation from the background or allowing content to be scrolled under another element.

levels.name: Tw Ring Shadow
levels.description: Tailwind default ring shadow placeholder - transparent shadow used as base for ring utilities

levels.name: Img Shadow
levels.description: Large diffuse shadow with 40% black opacity and 8px vertical offset, 30px blur - used for prominent image elevation

levels.name: Card Shadow
levels.description: Multi-layer shadow using CSS variables for border colors - 4 shadow layers creating subtle depth with decreasing opacity

levels.name: Inset Highlight
levels.description: Inset shadow using error background color for hover state highlighting

insights: The design system uses CSS custom properties extensively (--color-*, --radius-*, --btn-*) enabling theming flexibility and consistent token application across components.
Navigation employs glassmorphism with backdrop-blur-md (12px) and 60-70% opacity backgrounds for a modern layered depth effect while maintaining content legibility.
The tabbed carousel uses a unique progress-bar indicator pattern with auto-advance (6s timer) rather than traditional selected-state underlines, creating engagement through motion.
Button interactions rely primarily on opacity changes (hover:opacity-85) rather than background color shifts, maintaining visual consistency while providing clear feedback.

button_list.name: Download Warp (Primary CTA)
button_list.usage: Primary call-to-action button used in the navigation header and hero section for downloading the application.

button_list.name: Contact sales (Ghost)
button_list.usage: Secondary action button for enterprise sales inquiries, appears in navigation alongside primary CTA.

button_list.name: Products (Nav Dropdown)
button_list.usage: Navigation dropdown trigger button for accessing product submenu items.

button_list.name: Icon Button (Circular Close)
button_list.usage: Icon-only circular button for close actions in modals, announcements, and mobile menu.

button_list.name: GitHub Stars (Badge Button)
button_list.usage: External link button displaying GitHub star count, used for social proof in navigation.

button_list.name: Learn More (Inline Link)
button_list.usage: Inline text link used within announcement bars and body content for navigation to related content.

button_group.description: Buttons are arranged horizontally in navigation with consistent spacing for primary and secondary actions.

tabs.description: Tabs organize content into multiple sections with a progress bar indicator that fills horizontally to show the active state and auto-advancement timer.
when_to_use: Switching content categories in hero carousels

states.name: selected
